description: Findings that are ALWAYS rejected as standalone vulnerabilities. Each rule is matched against a finding's title, evidence, and (optionally) request/response snippets. If a rule matches, the candidate is killed with the rule's rejection_reason.

rules.description: Missing CSP header alone is informational, not a vulnerability.
Missing HSTS alone is informational.
Missing X-Frame-Options is informational; only matters if there is an active clickjacking PoC on a sensitive action.
Server version disclosure is informational unless chained to a known CVE.
Directory listing on a non-sensitive path is informational.
An open port is not a vulnerability. The vulnerability is whatever runs on the port.
Self-XSS where only the attacker can trigger the payload against their own account is not exploitable.
Reflected XSS on a logged-out page with no sensitive action to chain is rarely exploitable.
An open redirect is not exploitable on its own. It only matters when chained to OAuth, account takeover, or sensitive phishing flows.
SSRF that only reaches the agent's own infrastructure is internal-only and not a vulnerability.
Missing rate limit is not a vulnerability by itself. It must be chained to credential stuffing, account enumeration, or a high-value action.
CORS wildcard without credentials is not a vulnerability. With credentials, the browser blocks it. Chain to an authenticated sensitive action is required.
Mixed content on a non-sensitive asset is informational.
Missing cookie flags on a non-session cookie are informational.
Source map exposure is informational. It may reveal code paths but does not directly enable exploitation.
Email header disclosure of an internal field name is informational; chain to actual data exfiltration to escalate.
Stack trace disclosure is informational unless it contains a secret, internal hostname, or schema.
Swagger UI exposure is informational unless it reveals sensitive ops or secrets.
Default credentials on a public demo or sandbox endpoint are not vulnerabilities.
HTTP TRACE / wide OPTIONS alone is not exploitable without an XST chain.
